description_long: This object is a diverse collection of mushrooms, each with distinct cap shapes, sizes, and colors, arranged upright as if freshly foraged. The mushrooms display a variety of cap patterns, including smooth, spotted, and scaly surfaces, with some caps showing warty or cracked textures. Stems are present on most specimens, varying in thickness and length, and some mushrooms have visible gills beneath their caps. The color palette ranges from white and tan to deep brown, red, and yellow, with some caps featuring prominent white spots or textured surfaces. The mushrooms appear natural and organic, with irregularities and imperfections typical of wild fungi. The group is presented on a neutral, undefined background, suggesting no specific environmental context.
description: A varied group of wild mushrooms with different cap shapes, colors, and surface patterns, featuring visible stems and natural imperfections.
description_view_0: Front view shows the mushrooms lined up in four distinct groups, highlighting the diversity in cap shapes and surface patterns.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizes the cap textures and color variations, with the arrangement making the differences in cap diameter more apparent.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the undersides of some caps, showing partial gill visibility and the curvature of the stems.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, displaying the mushrooms' profiles and the irregularity in stem lengths and cap margins.
attribute_cap_pattern: spotted, smooth, scaly, warty, and cracked
